title: centrolepis glabra
intro: Centrolepis glabra is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant that belongs to the family Centrolepidaceae. It is native to New Zealand and has been used in traditional Maori medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been shown to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and preventing the growth of certain microorganisms. Herbal preparations of Centrolepis glabra include infusions and decoctions, which can be used to treat a variety of health issues, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential interactions with other medications.
